fsm_model.description: FSM modeling the Device Attestation procedure, including certificate validation, cryptographic verification, revocation checking, and policy validation

states.name: Unattested
states.description: Initial state before attestation procedure begins. Commissionee has DAC and private key, but Commissioner has not yet initiated attestation.

states.name: NonceGenerated
states.description: Commissioner has generated a fresh 32-byte random attestation nonce using Crypto_DRBG.

states.name: AttestationRequested
states.description: Commissioner has sent AttestationRequest with nonce to Commissionee, awaiting AttestationResponse.

states.name: ResponseReceived
states.description: Commissionee returned AttestationResponse with signed Attestation Information. Ready for validation.

states.name: CertificateChainObtained
states.description: Commissioner has obtained DAC chain (DAC, PAI, PAA) either before or after receiving AttestationResponse.

states.name: NonceValidated
states.description: AttestationNonce in response matches the Commissioner's provided nonce.

states.name: SignatureValidated
states.description: Device Attestation Signature has been cryptographically verified using DAC public key.

states.name: CertificateFormatValidated
states.description: DAC, PAI, PAA certificates conform to all format constraints (version, encoding, extensions, key usage, basic constraints, etc.)

states.name: ChainCryptographicallyValidated
states.description: Certificate chain (DAC→PAI→PAA) has been validated using Crypto_VerifyChainDER with respect to DAC notBefore timestamp.

states.name: PAAValidated
states.description: PAA certificate is present in Commissioner's trusted root store and was in DCL at DAC issuance time.

states.name: RevocationChecked
states.description: DAC and PAI revocation status has been checked against DCL-based revocation data. Neither certificate is revoked.

states.name: VendorIDPolicyValidated
states.description: VendorID consistency validated across entire chain (DAC, PAI, PAA if present) and matches Certification Declaration.

states.name: ProductIDPolicyValidated
states.description: ProductID consistency validated across chain and matches Certification Declaration product_id_array or dac_origin_product_id.

states.name: CertificationDeclarationValidated
states.description: Certification Declaration fully validated: VID/PID match Basic Info cluster, dac_origin fields consistent, authorized_paa_list checked, certificate_id matched (SHOULD).

states.name: AttestationChallengeValidated
states.description: AttestationChallenge from secure session (CASE/PASE) context matched in Attestation Information.

states.name: FirmwareInfoValidated
states.description: firmware_information field (if present) matched against DCL DeviceSoftwareCompliance entry. Optional validation.

states.name: FullyAttested
states.description: Device successfully attested. All validations passed. Commissioner can proceed with commissioning.

states.name: RequestTimeout
states.description: AttestationResponse not received within timeout period.

states.name: NonceValidationFailed
states.description: AttestationNonce in response does not match Commissioner's nonce. Possible replay attack.

states.name: SignatureVerificationFailed
states.description: Device Attestation Signature verification failed. Device does not possess private key or signature is invalid.

states.name: CertificateFormatInvalid
states.description: One or more certificates in chain do not conform to format requirements (wrong version, encoding, extensions, key usage, etc.).

states.name: ChainValidationFailed
states.description: Cryptographic chain validation failed (invalid signatures, issuer-subject mismatch, wrong path length, etc.).

states.name: PAANotTrusted
states.description: PAA certificate not found in Commissioner's trusted root store or not in DCL at DAC issuance time.

states.name: CertificateRevoked
states.description: DAC or PAI certificate found in revocation list. Device credentials compromised or recalled.

states.name: RevocationCheckSkipped
states.description: Revocation data unavailable. Commissioner may proceed with warning to user about potential risk.

states.name: CRLStaleWarning
states.description: CRL data available but no update beyond nextUpdate timestamp. Commissioner proceeds with user warning.

states.name: CertificateIDMismatched
states.description: certificate_id field does not match DCL CDCertificateID (non-fatal SHOULD validation). Commissioner proceeds with additional DCL check.

states.name: VendorIDPolicyViolation
states.description: VendorID mismatch across chain or with Certification Declaration. Cross-vendor attack detected.

states.name: ProductIDPolicyViolation
states.description: ProductID mismatch in chain or not in Certification Declaration product_id_array.

states.name: CertificationDeclarationInvalid
states.description: Certification Declaration validation failed (VID/PID mismatch with Basic Info, dac_origin inconsistency, unauthorized PAA, etc.).

states.name: EncodingAmbiguityDetected
states.description: VendorID/ProductID encoding methods mixed within same field or malformed fallback encoding detected.

states.name: OfflineCommissioning
states.description: Commissioning proceeds without DCL access. Commissioner validates CD against runtime attributes but cannot verify certificate_id or firmware info.

transitions.guard_condition: validate_dac_format() == true && validate_pai_format() == true && validate_paa_format() == true
validate_dac_format() == false || validate_pai_format() == false || validate_paa_format() == false
crypto_verify_chain_der(dac_certificate, pai_certificate, paa_certificate, dac_notBefore) == SUCCESS && verify_issuer_subject_matches() == true && path_length == 2
crypto_verify_chain_der(dac_certificate, pai_certificate, paa_certificate, dac_notBefore) == FAILURE || verify_issuer_subject_matches() == false || path_length != 2
revocation_data_available == true && is_revoked(dac_certificate, revocation_set) == false && is_revoked(pai_certificate, revocation_set) == false
revocation_data_available == true && (is_revoked(dac_certificate, revocation_set) == true || is_revoked(pai_certificate, revocation_set) == true)
revocation_data_available == true && crl_is_stale() == true && no_revocations_found() == true

extract_vid(dac_certificate) == extract_vid(pai_certificate) && (has_vid(paa_certificate) == false || extract_vid(paa_certificate) == extract_vid(pai_certificate)) && encoding_method_consistent() == true

extract_vid(dac_certificate) != extract_vid(pai_certificate) || (has_vid(paa_certificate) == true && extract_vid(paa_certificate) != extract_vid(pai_certificate)) || encoding_method_consistent() == false

extract_vid(dac_certificate) == extract_vid(pai_certificate) && (has_vid(paa_certificate) == false || extract_vid(paa_certificate) == extract_vid(pai_certificate)) && encoding_method_consistent() == true

extract_vid(dac_certificate) != extract_vid(pai_certificate) || (has_vid(paa_certificate) == true && extract_vid(paa_certificate) != extract_vid(pai_certificate)) || encoding_method_consistent() == false

extract_vid(dac_certificate) == extract_vid(pai_certificate) && (has_vid(paa_certificate) == false || extract_vid(paa_certificate) == extract_vid(pai_certificate)) && encoding_method_consistent() == true

extract_vid(dac_certificate) != extract_vid(pai_certificate) || (has_vid(paa_certificate) == true && extract_vid(paa_certificate) != extract_vid(pai_certificate)) || encoding_method_consistent() == false
cd_vid_matches_basic_info() == true && cd_pid_in_array() == true && validate_dac_origin_fields() == true && validate_authorized_paa_list() == true
cd_vid_matches_basic_info() == false || cd_pid_in_array() == false || validate_dac_origin_fields() == false || validate_authorized_paa_list() == false
cd_present == true && cd_vid_matches_basic_info() == true && cd_pid_in_array() == true

functions.name: generate_random_nonce
functions.description: Generates a cryptographically secure random 32-byte attestation nonce
functions.algorithm_detail: Crypto_DRBG(): Uses deterministic random bit generator compliant with cryptographic standards. Output is 32 bytes (256 bits) of high-entropy random data suitable for nonce/challenge generation. Must be unpredictable and have sufficient entropy to prevent replay attacks.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in Unattested→NonceGenerated transition to create fresh attestation nonce for each attestation attempt

functions.name: send_message

parameters.name: nonce
parameters.description: Attestation nonce to include in request

functions.description: Sends AttestationRequest message to Commissionee over secure session containing the attestation nonce
functions.algorithm_detail: Protocol message transmission over established CASE/PASE secure session. Message includes attestation_nonce field set to generated nonce value.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in NonceGenerated→AttestationRequested transition to send attestation request to device

functions.name: extract_attestation_elements
functions.description: Extracts and parses the attestation elements structure from AttestationResponse message
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse TLV/protocol-encoded message structure to extract attestation_elements field containing certification_declaration, attestation_nonce, firmware_information (optional), etc.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when receiving AttestationResponse to extract elements for validation

functions.name: extract_signature
functions.description: Extracts the Device Attestation Signature from AttestationResponse
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse message to extract attestation_signature field. Signature is ECDSA signature over attestation TBS using device's DAC private key.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when receiving AttestationResponse to extract signature for cryptographic verification

functions.name: extract_tbs
functions.description: Extracts or reconstructs the attestation to-be-signed (TBS) data that was signed by device
functions.algorithm_detail: Extract or reconstruct the exact byte sequence that was signed by device: attestation_elements structure as specified in Section 11.18.4.7. Must match byte-for-byte what device signed.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when receiving AttestationResponse to obtain TBS data for signature verification

functions.name: extract_cd
functions.description: Extracts Certification Declaration from attestation elements
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse attestation_elements to extract certification_declaration field containing vendor_id, product_id_array, dac_origin fields (optional), authorized_paa_list (optional), certificate_id, etc.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when receiving AttestationResponse to extract CD for policy validation

functions.name: request_certificate
functions.description: Requests certificate from Commissionee using CertificateChainRequest or retrieves from cache
functions.algorithm_detail: Send CertificateChainRequest message specifying certificate type (1=DAC, 2=PAI). PAA obtained from Commissioner's trust store or DCL. Returns DER-encoded X.509v3 certificate.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ResponseReceived→CertificateChainObtained transition to obtain certificate chain for validation

functions.name: extract_nonce
functions.description: Extracts attestation nonce from attestation elements structure
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse attestation_elements structure to extract attestation_nonce field value (32 bytes).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in CertificateChainObtained→NonceValidated transition to check nonce match

functions.name: crypto_verify
functions.description: Cryptographically verifies ECDSA signature using public key from DAC
functions.algorithm_detail: Crypto_Verify(): ECDSA signature verification using curve prime256v1 (secp256r1, NIST P-256) with SHA256 hash. Verify that signature was created by private key corresponding to public_key over message. Returns SUCCESS if signature valid, FAILURE otherwise. Algorithm per RFC 5280 and ECDSA specifications.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in NonceValidated→SignatureValidated transition to prove device possesses DAC private key

functions.name: validate_dac_format
functions.description: Validates DAC certificate conforms to all format constraints
functions.algorithm_detail: Check DAC constraints per Section 6.2.2.3: version=v3(2), DER-encoded, signature=ecdsa-with-SHA256, issuer sequence with exactly one VID and zero-or-one PID, issuer byte-for-byte matches PAI subject, subject sequence with exactly one VID and one PID, VID in subject matches VID in issuer, PID in subject matches PID in issuer (if present), algorithm=prime256v1, BasicConstraints critical with cA=FALSE, KeyUsage critical with only digitalSignature, required extensions present. Returns true if all constraints satisfied.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in SignatureValidated→CertificateFormatValidated transition to validate DAC format

functions.name: validate_pai_format
functions.description: Validates PAI certificate conforms to all format constraints
functions.algorithm_detail: Check PAI constraints per Section 6.2.2.4: version=v3(2), signature=ecdsa-with-SHA256, issuer sequence with zero-or-one VID, issuer byte-for-byte matches PAA subject, subject sequence with exactly one VID, VID in subject matches VID in issuer (if issuer has VID), zero-or-one PID in subject, algorithm=prime256v1, BasicConstraints critical with cA=TRUE and pathLen=0, KeyUsage critical with keyCertSign and cRLSign set (digitalSignature MAY be set, others NOT set), required extensions present. Returns true if all constraints satisfied.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in SignatureValidated→CertificateFormatValidated transition to validate PAI format

functions.name: validate_paa_format
functions.description: Validates PAA certificate conforms to all format constraints
functions.algorithm_detail: Check PAA constraints per Section 6.2.2.5: version=v3(2), signature=ecdsa-with-SHA256, issuer and subject match exactly (self-signed), issuer and subject have zero-or-one VID, no PID in issuer or subject, algorithm=prime256v1, BasicConstraints critical with cA=TRUE and pathLen=1 (if present), KeyUsage critical with keyCertSign and cRLSign set (digitalSignature MAY be set, others NOT set), required extensions present. Returns true if all constraints satisfied.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in SignatureValidated→CertificateFormatValidated transition to validate PAA format

functions.name: crypto_verify_chain_der
functions.description: Validates certificate chain cryptographically using RFC 5280 path validation
functions.algorithm_detail: Crypto_VerifyChainDER(): Perform RFC 5280 certificate path validation. Verify: (1) PAA signature on PAI using PAA public key, (2) PAI signature on DAC using PAI public key, (3) certificate validity periods at validation_time (DAC notBefore), (4) path length exactly 2 (3 certificates: PAA, PAI, DAC), (5) issuer-subject byte-for-byte matches between certificates. Returns SUCCESS if chain valid, FAILURE otherwise.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in CertificateFormatValidated→ChainCryptographicallyValidated transition to validate certificate chain signatures

functions.name: verify_issuer_subject_matches
functions.description: Verifies issuer field of each certificate matches subject field of issuing certificate byte-for-byte
functions.algorithm_detail: Check: DAC.issuer == PAI.subject (byte-for-byte) AND PAI.issuer == PAA.subject (byte-for-byte). Must match exactly including encoding, attribute order, whitespace. Per RFC 5280 name matching requirements.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in CertificateFormatValidated→ChainCryptographicallyValidated transition to validate chain structure

functions.name: paa_in_trusted_store
functions.description: Checks if PAA certificate is present in Commissioner's trusted root certificate store
functions.algorithm_detail: Compare PAA certificate (by subject and SKI) against Commissioner's list of trusted PAA root certificates. Trusted store SHOULD include PAAs from Distributed Compliance Ledger. Returns true if found.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ChainCryptographicallyValidated→PAAValidated transition to validate trust anchor

functions.name: paa_in_dcl_at_time
functions.description: Checks if PAA was present in Distributed Compliance Ledger at specified historical timestamp
functions.algorithm_detail: Query DCL for PAA certificates that were valid/approved at the given timestamp. Compare PAA certificate against historical DCL state at DAC issuance time (notBefore). Returns true if PAA was in approved list at that time.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ChainCryptographicallyValidated→PAAValidated transition to validate PAA was trusted at issuance

functions.name: is_revoked
functions.description: Determines if certificate is revoked using DCL-based revocation data
functions.algorithm_detail: Per Section 6.2.4.2: Extract certificate's issuer AKI and serial number. Query revocation_set indexed by (CertificateAuthorityKeyIdentifier, CertificateAuthorityName). Check if serial number is in revoked set. Handle CRL delegation: for DAC, issuer must match CRLSignerCertificate or CRLSignerDelegator; for PAI, issuer must match PAA that signed CRLSignerCertificate. Returns true if revoked.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in PAAValidated→RevocationChecked transition to check if DAC or PAI is revoked

functions.name: extract_vid
functions.description: Extracts VendorID from certificate subject using preferred or fallback method
functions.algorithm_detail: Per Section 6.2.2.2: First check if Matter OID 1.3.6.1.4.1.37244.2.1 present in any RDN - if yes, use that value (preferred method). If OID not present, parse commonName for 'Mvid:XXXX' pattern (fallback method): find leftmost match of 'Mvid:' followed by exactly 4 uppercase hex digits, convert to 16-bit integer. If prefix found but no valid match, field is invalid. Returns VendorID value.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in RevocationChecked→VendorIDPolicyValidated transition to extract and compare VIDs

functions.name: extract_pid
functions.description: Extracts ProductID from certificate subject using preferred or fallback method
functions.algorithm_detail: Per Section 6.2.2.2: First check if Matter OID 1.3.6.1.4.1.37244.2.2 present in any RDN - if yes, use that value (preferred method). If OID not present, parse commonName for 'Mpid:XXXX' pattern (fallback method): find leftmost match of 'Mpid:' followed by exactly 4 uppercase hex digits, convert to 16-bit integer. If prefix found but no valid match, field is invalid. Returns ProductID value.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in VendorIDPolicyValidated→ProductIDPolicyValidated transition to extract and compare PIDs

functions.name: has_vid
functions.description: Checks if certificate subject contains a VendorID value
functions.algorithm_detail: Check if Matter VID OID present or valid 'Mvid:XXXX' pattern found in commonName. Returns true if VID present.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called to determine if PAA has optional VID for validation logic branching

functions.name: has_pid
functions.description: Checks if certificate subject contains a ProductID value
functions.algorithm_detail: Check if Matter PID OID present or valid 'Mpid:XXXX' pattern found in commonName. Returns true if PID present.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called to determine if PAI has optional PID for validation logic branching

functions.name: encoding_method_consistent
functions.description: Validates VID/PID encoding methods are not mixed within same field and fallback method is correctly formatted
functions.algorithm_detail: Per Section 6.2.2.2: For each subject/issuer field, check: (1) If Matter OID present, fallback method SHALL NOT be used for that field, (2) No mixing of preferred and fallback within same field, (3) If fallback used: exactly 4 uppercase hex digits, leftmost valid match used, if prefix present without valid match then invalid. Returns true if encoding valid.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in VendorIDPolicyValidated transition to detect encoding ambiguity attacks

functions.name: cd_vid_matches_basic_info
functions.description: Validates vendor_id field in Certification Declaration matches VendorID attribute from Basic Information cluster
functions.algorithm_detail: Per Section 6.2.3.1: Read VendorID attribute from Basic Information cluster. Compare to certification_declaration.vendor_id field. Must match exactly. Returns true if match.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ProductIDPolicyValidated→CertificationDeclarationValidated transition for CD validation

functions.name: cd_pid_in_array
functions.description: Validates ProductID attribute from Basic Information cluster is in product_id_array field of Certification Declaration
functions.algorithm_detail: Per Section 6.2.3.1: Read ProductID attribute from Basic Information cluster. Check if value is present in certification_declaration.product_id_array list. Returns true if found.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ProductIDPolicyValidated→CertificationDeclarationValidated transition for CD validation

functions.name: validate_dac_origin_fields
functions.description: Validates dac_origin_vendor_id and dac_origin_product_id fields consistency in Certification Declaration
functions.algorithm_detail: Per Section 6.2.3.1: Check CD has both or neither dac_origin fields. If both present: DAC VID must match dac_origin_vendor_id, PAI VID must match dac_origin_vendor_id, DAC PID must match dac_origin_product_id, PAI PID (if present) must match dac_origin_product_id. If neither present: DAC VID must match vendor_id, PAI VID must match vendor_id, DAC PID must be in product_id_array, PAI PID (if present) must be in product_id_array. Returns true if valid.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ProductIDPolicyValidated→CertificationDeclarationValidated transition for ODM scenario validation

functions.name: validate_authorized_paa_list
functions.description: Validates PAA SKI is in authorized_paa_list field if field is present in Certification Declaration
functions.algorithm_detail: Per Section 6.2.3.1: If certification_declaration.authorized_paa_list field present, extract PAA certificate's Subject Key Identifier extension value. Check if SKI is present in authorized_paa_list array. If field not present, validation passes. Returns true if valid or field absent.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ProductIDPolicyValidated→CertificationDeclarationValidated transition for authorized PAA checking

functions.name: get_basic_info_attribute
functions.description: Reads specified attribute from device's Basic Information cluster
functions.algorithm_detail: Query Matter cluster data model: read attribute from Basic Information cluster (cluster ID 0x0028). Attributes: VendorID (0x0002, 16-bit), ProductID (0x0004, 16-bit), SoftwareVersion (0x000A, 32-bit). Returns attribute value.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in CertificationDeclarationValidated transition to get device runtime attributes for CD comparison

functions.name: extract_challenge
functions.description: Extracts AttestationChallenge from attestation elements structure
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse attestation_elements to extract attestation_challenge field. Value depends on session context.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in CertificationDeclarationValidated→AttestationChallengeValidated transition

functions.name: get_session_challenge
functions.description: Obtains AttestationChallenge from secure session context
functions.algorithm_detail: Per Section 6.2.3.1: AttestationChallenge SHALL be obtained from CASE session, resumed CASE session, or PASE session depending on commissioning method. Extract challenge value from session security context.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in CertificationDeclarationValidated→AttestationChallengeValidated transition to get expected challenge

functions.name: has_firmware_info
functions.description: Checks if firmware_information field is present in attestation elements
functions.algorithm_detail: Check if optional firmware_information field present in attestation_elements structure. Returns true if present.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in AttestationChallengeValidated→FirmwareInfoValidated transition for conditional validation

functions.name: firmware_info_matches_dcl
functions.description: Validates firmware_information field matches DCL DeviceSoftwareCompliance entry
functions.algorithm_detail: Per Section 6.2.3.1: If firmware_information present, compare against DCL entry for device (matched by VendorID, ProductID, SoftwareVersion from Basic Info). Returns true if match or if Commissioner does not support firmware validation.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in AttestationChallengeValidated→FirmwareInfoValidated transition for firmware validation

functions.name: query_dcl_firmware_info
functions.description: Queries Distributed Compliance Ledger for firmware information matching device identity
functions.algorithm_detail: Query DCL DeviceSoftwareCompliance schema with (VendorID, ProductID, SoftwareVersion) tuple. Returns firmware_information field from matching entry, or null if not found or DCL unavailable.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in AttestationChallengeValidated→FirmwareInfoValidated transition to get expected firmware info

functions.name: extract_firmware_info_if_present
functions.description: Extracts firmware_information field if present, returns null if absent
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse attestation_elements for optional firmware_information field. Return field value or null.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in AttestationChallengeValidated→FirmwareInfoValidated transition

functions.name: current_timestamp
functions.description: Returns current system timestamp
functions.algorithm_detail: Get current time from system clock. Used for timeout tracking and logging.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called for timestamping attestation events

functions.name: extract_subject
functions.description: Extracts subject field from X.509 certificate
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse DER-encoded certificate to extract subject field (X.500 Distinguished Name).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called for error reporting when PAA not trusted

functions.name: extract_ski
functions.description: Extracts Subject Key Identifier extension from certificate
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse certificate extensions to extract SKI extension value (octet string).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called for PAA identification and authorized PAA list checking

functions.name: extract_notBefore
functions.description: Extracts notBefore timestamp from certificate validity period
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse certificate validity field to extract notBefore value (start of validity period).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called to get DAC issuance time for temporal validation

functions.name: identify_invalid_cert
functions.description: Identifies which certificate (DAC, PAI, PAA) failed format validation
functions.algorithm_detail: Determine which certificate validation function returned false. Returns 'DAC', 'PAI', or 'PAA'.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called for error reporting in format validation failures

functions.name: identify_violation
functions.description: Identifies specific format constraint that was violated
functions.algorithm_detail: Return description of which format constraint failed (e.g., 'wrong version', 'cA field incorrect', 'missing extension', 'wrong key usage').
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called for detailed error reporting in format validation failures

functions.name: identify_chain_error
functions.description: Identifies specific chain validation error
functions.algorithm_detail: Return description of chain validation failure (e.g., 'signature invalid', 'issuer-subject mismatch', 'wrong path length', 'validity period expired').
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called for error reporting in chain validation failures

functions.name: identify_revoked_cert
functions.description: Identifies which certificate (DAC or PAI) was found revoked
functions.algorithm_detail: Determine which certificate is in revocation list. Returns 'DAC' or 'PAI'.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called for error reporting when revocation detected

functions.name: extract_revocation_reason
functions.description: Extracts revocation reason from CRL entry if available
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse CRL entry extensions to extract revocation reason code if present (per RFC 5280 section 5.3.1). Returns reason string or 'unspecified'.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called for detailed error reporting when certificate revoked

functions.name: identify_skip_reason
functions.description: Identifies reason why revocation check was skipped
functions.algorithm_detail: Determine why revocation data unavailable: 'dcl_unavailable', 'crl_stale', 'network_error', etc.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when revocation check skipped to report reason

functions.name: notify_user
functions.description: Notifies user of security warning or bypass condition
functions.algorithm_detail: Display warning message to user interface. Per spec SHOULD notify when revocation check skipped or CRL stale.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when security checks are bypassed to inform user of potential risks

functions.name: identify_vid_mismatch
functions.description: Identifies where VendorID mismatch occurred in chain
functions.algorithm_detail: Determine which VID comparison failed: 'dac_pai_mismatch', 'pai_paa_mismatch', 'mixed_encoding', 'cd_mismatch'.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called for error reporting in VID policy violations

functions.name: identify_pid_mismatch
functions.description: Identifies where ProductID mismatch occurred
functions.algorithm_detail: Determine which PID comparison failed: 'dac_pai_mismatch', 'cd_mismatch', 'mixed_encoding'.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called for error reporting in PID policy violations

functions.name: identify_cd_error
functions.description: Identifies specific Certification Declaration validation error
functions.algorithm_detail: Determine which CD validation failed: 'vid_mismatch', 'pid_not_in_array', 'dac_origin_inconsistent', 'unauthorized_paa', 'certificate_id_mismatch'.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called for error reporting in CD validation failures

functions.name: generate_event
functions.description: Generates system event for logging or notification
functions.algorithm_detail: Create event record with timestamp and event type for system logging, audit trail, or triggering callbacks.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called throughout FSM to record significant events and errors

functions.name: get_session_type
functions.description: Determines type of secure session (CASE, PASE, resumed CASE)
functions.algorithm_detail: Query session context to determine session establishment method. Returns 'CASE', 'PASE', or 'resumed_CASE'.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called for session type reporting and validation context

functions.name: generate_format_report
functions.description: Generates detailed format validation result report
functions.algorithm_detail: Compile results of all format checks into structured report for logging or diagnostics.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when format validation succeeds to record validation details

functions.name: extract_vid_if_present
functions.description: Extracts VendorID if present, returns null if not present
functions.algorithm_detail: Call extract_vid() if has_vid() returns true, otherwise return null.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called for PAA VID extraction which is optional

functions.name: extract_pid_if_present
functions.description: Extracts ProductID if present, returns null if not present
functions.algorithm_detail: Call extract_pid() if has_pid() returns true, otherwise return null.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called for PAI PID extraction which is optional

functions.name: pid_encoding_consistent
functions.description: Validates ProductID encoding methods are not mixed within same field
functions.algorithm_detail: Similar to encoding_method_consistent() but specifically for ProductID. Check no mixing of preferred and fallback methods within same field.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in ProductID policy validation to detect encoding ambiguity

functions.name: crl_is_stale
functions.description: Determines if CRL data is stale (beyond nextUpdate timestamp without newer version available)
functions.algorithm_detail: Per Section 6.2.4.2: Check if current time is beyond nextUpdate field in CRL and no updated CRL is available. Returns true if CRL is stale.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in revocation checking to detect stale CRL data requiring user notification

functions.name: no_revocations_found
functions.description: Checks if revocation check found no revoked certificates despite CRL being stale
functions.algorithm_detail: Returns true if neither DAC nor PAI found in revocation set. Used to determine whether Commissioner can proceed with stale CRL warning vs hard failure.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called with crl_is_stale() to determine appropriate path when CRL data is outdated

functions.name: extract_crl_next_update
functions.description: Extracts nextUpdate timestamp from CRL data
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse CRL structure to extract nextUpdate field value (GeneralizedTime). Indicates when CA expects to publish next CRL update.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when CRL is stale to record expected update time for user notification

functions.name: certificate_id_matches_dcl
functions.description: Validates certificate_id field in Certification Declaration matches CDCertificateID in DCL DeviceSoftwareCompliance entry
functions.algorithm_detail: Per Section 6.2.3.1: Query DCL DeviceSoftwareCompliance schema with (VendorID, ProductID, SoftwareVersion) from Basic Info. Compare CD certificate_id with CDCertificateID field from matching entry. SHOULD validation (non-fatal). Returns true if match or DCL entry not found.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called after CD validation to perform SHOULD-level certificate_id consistency check

functions.name: query_dcl_certificate_id
functions.description: Queries DCL for CDCertificateID matching device identity
functions.algorithm_detail: Query DCL DeviceSoftwareCompliance schema with (VendorID, ProductID, SoftwareVersion) tuple. Returns CDCertificateID field from matching entry, or null if not found or DCL unavailable.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when certificate_id mismatch detected to retrieve expected value

functions.name: extract_certificate_id
functions.description: Extracts certificate_id field from Certification Declaration
functions.algorithm_detail: Parse CD structure to extract certificate_id field value (string identifier).
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called when validating certificate_id to extract CD value for comparison

functions.name: software_version_is_certified_in_dcl
functions.description: Checks if device's current software version has entry in DCL DeviceSoftwareCompliance schema
functions.algorithm_detail: Per Section 6.2.3.1: When certificate_id doesn't match (e.g., firmware updated but CD not updated), Commissioner SHOULD check DCL for entry with matching (VendorID, ProductID, SoftwareVersion) to validate current version is certified. Returns true if certified entry found.
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called from CertificateIDMismatched state to determine if software version is certified despite CD mismatch

definitions.definition: X.509v3 certificate unique to each Matter device that proves the device was legitimately manufactured by a certified vendor. Contains device's public key and VendorID/ProductID information.
definitions.security_relevance: Foundation of device authentication. Compromise of DAC private key enables device impersonation.

definitions.definition: X.509v3 intermediate CA certificate assigned to a vendor (and optionally a specific product) that signs DACs for that vendor's devices.
definitions.security_relevance: Compromise of PAI private key enables creation of fraudulent devices for entire product line or vendor.

definitions.definition: Self-signed X.509v3 root CA certificate that forms the trust anchor for device attestation. Signs PAI certificates.
definitions.security_relevance: Trust anchor for entire attestation PKI. PAAs must be carefully managed and their private keys highly protected.

definitions.definition: 32-byte cryptographically secure random value generated by Commissioner for each attestation attempt to prevent replay attacks.
definitions.security_relevance: Ensures attestation response freshness. Non-random or reused nonces enable replay attacks.

definitions.definition: Data structure containing certified product information including vendor_id, product_id_array, optional dac_origin fields, authorized_paa_list, and certificate_id.
definitions.security_relevance: Binds device attestation to certification process. Validates device is certified product running certified firmware.

definitions.definition: Decentralized ledger containing trusted PAA certificates, revocation information, and device compliance data.
definitions.security_relevance: Source of trust anchors and revocation data. DCL compromise or unavailability impacts attestation security.

definitions.definition: Challenge value from secure session context (CASE/PASE) that binds attestation to specific commissioning session.
definitions.security_relevance: Prevents attestation response replay across different sessions or contexts.

definitions.definition: Encoding VendorID using Matter-specific OID 1.3.6.1.4.1.37244.2.1 as AttributeTypeAndValue entry in certificate subject/issuer.
definitions.security_relevance: Unambiguous identification method. Reduces parsing complexity and encoding attack surface.

definitions.definition: Encoding VendorID as 'Mvid:' followed by exactly 4 uppercase hexadecimal digits anywhere within commonName attribute.
definitions.security_relevance: Backward compatibility method. More complex parsing creates potential for ambiguity attacks if not strictly validated.

definitions.definition: Similar to VendorID: preferred method uses OID 1.3.6.1.4.1.37244.2.2, fallback uses 'Mpid:XXXX' pattern in commonName.
definitions.security_relevance: Product identity binding. Encoding ambiguity could enable cross-product attacks.

definitions.definition: Optional fields in Certification Declaration (dac_origin_vendor_id, dac_origin_product_id) used when DAC issuer differs from certified vendor (ODM manufacturing scenario).
definitions.security_relevance: Enables ODM manufacturing model while maintaining vendor accountability. Must be validated consistently.

definitions.definition: Optional field in Certification Declaration restricting which PAAs are authorized to certify the product. Contains list of PAA Subject Key Identifiers.
definitions.security_relevance: Additional access control layer. Prevents certification from compromised or untrusted PAAs.

definitions.definition: Data structure built from DCL containing CRL-based revocation information indexed by certificate authority identity and serial numbers of revoked certificates.
definitions.security_relevance: Enables detection of compromised or recalled certificates. Unavailability creates security gap.

definitions.definition: Mechanism allowing PAI/PAA to delegate CRL signing to other entities. CRL signer certificate validated through delegation chain.
definitions.security_relevance: Operational flexibility but adds validation complexity. Delegation chain must be validated to prevent fraudulent revocation data.

definitions.definition: Cryptographically secure deterministic random bit generator function used for nonce generation.
definitions.security_relevance: Source of randomness for challenge-response. Weak DRBG enables nonce prediction and replay attacks.

definitions.definition: ECDSA signature verification function using prime256v1 curve and SHA256 hash.
definitions.security_relevance: Core cryptographic primitive for attestation. Implementation bugs enable signature forgery.

definitions.definition: RFC 5280 certificate chain validation function verifying chain structure, signatures, and validity periods.
definitions.security_relevance: Validates entire trust chain. Incorrect implementation enables fraudulent certificate acceptance.

definitions.definition: Matter data model cluster (0x0028) containing device metadata including VendorID, ProductID, and SoftwareVersion attributes.
definitions.security_relevance: Runtime device identity. Must match Certification Declaration. Manipulation could enable false certification claims.

definitions.definition: Secure session (CASE or PASE) established between Commissioner and Commissionee for device commissioning including attestation.
definitions.security_relevance: Provides confidentiality and integrity for attestation exchange. Session security protects against MITM attacks.

definitions.definition: Field in Certification Declaration that SHOULD match CDCertificateID from DCL DeviceSoftwareCompliance entry. Used to bind CD to specific certified firmware version.
definitions.security_relevance: Enables detection of outdated CDs after firmware updates. Mismatch triggers additional software version certification check. Non-fatal SHOULD validation.

definitions.definition: RFC 5280 certificate extension pointing to CRL locations. Present in Device Attestation certificates but SHALL be ignored by Matter Commissioners.
definitions.security_relevance: Ignored to enforce DCL as single source of revocation data. Ensures consistent, interoperable revocation handling across Matter ecosystem.

definitions.definition: Condition when current time exceeds CRL nextUpdate field without newer CRL available. Commissioner MAY proceed with user warning.
definitions.security_relevance: Trade-off between availability and security. Stale CRL may miss recent revocations but blocking commissioning affects usability. User notification required.

definitions.definition: Commissioning scenario when DCL is unavailable. Commissioner validates CD against runtime attributes but defers certificate_id and software version certification checks.
definitions.security_relevance: Enables commissioning without internet access but reduces validation completeness. Valid CD ensures device was previously certified. Deferred DCL check SHOULD occur later.

definitions.definition: Critical CRL extension required when multiple CRL entries exist for same CA in DCL. Contains uniformResourceIdentifier matching DCL DataUrl field.
definitions.security_relevance: Prevents CRL confusion attacks when CA has multiple distribution points. Binds specific CRL to specific DCL entry through URI matching.

security_properties.description: Device proves possession of DAC private key through cryptographic signature verification, ensuring device is authentic and possesses claimed identity
Fresh random nonce prevents replay of captured attestation responses. Each attestation attempt uses unique nonce.
Complete cryptographic chain validation from DAC through PAI to trusted PAA root ensures device credentials issued by legitimate authority
Revoked certificates detected and rejected using DCL-based revocation data, preventing use of compromised credentials
VendorID consistency enforced across certificate chain prevents cross-vendor certificate misuse and maintains vendor accountability
Device identity (VID/PID from certificates) bound to Certification Declaration and Basic Info cluster ensures only certified products commission
Attestation bound to specific secure session through AttestationChallenge prevents cross-session replay
Strict certificate format validation prevents bypass through malformed certificates or policy-violating constraints
Certificate validity periods checked at DAC issuance time ensures certificates were valid when issued
VID/PID encoding method consistency enforced prevents encoding ambiguity attacks through mixed or malformed encodings
dac_origin fields enable ODM manufacturing while maintaining vendor accountability through explicit origin declaration
authorized_paa_list restricts which PAAs can certify product, preventing certification from compromised or untrusted roots
Optional firmware_information validation ensures device runs certified firmware version
certificate_id SHOULD match DCL CDCertificateID. Mismatch triggers software version certification check to handle firmware update scenarios.
Commissioner handles stale CRL data gracefully by proceeding with user notification, balancing security and availability
Enables commissioning without DCL access when valid CD present. Defers non-critical checks while ensuring basic certification validation.
Commissioner SHOULD notify user when security validations bypassed or degraded (revocation unavailable, CRL stale, offline mode)

assumptions: DRBG is cryptographically secure and properly seeded
System entropy source provides sufficient randomness
Nonce generation is not predictable or repeatable
ECDSA implementation is correct and timing-attack resistant
Device private key is secure and not compromised
Certificate parsing is secure and handles malformed input
No exploitable parsing vulnerabilities in DER decoder
Validation timestamp is accurate and not manipulated

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: OID parsing or regex pattern matching
assumptions: Preferred method OID parsing is unambiguous
Fallback method regex strictly enforces format (4 uppercase hex)
No encoding method mixing within same field

security_assumptions.assumption: Device private key for DAC is securely stored and cannot be extracted
security_assumptions.if_violated: Device impersonation attacks become possible through key cloning

security_assumptions.assumption: Commissioner has access to Distributed Compliance Ledger for PAA trust anchors and revocation data
security_assumptions.if_violated: Cannot validate PAA trust or check revocation, enabling compromised certificate acceptance

security_assumptions.assumption: Distributed Compliance Ledger is trustworthy and not compromised
security_assumptions.if_violated: Fraudulent PAAs could be trusted, revocation data could be manipulated

security_assumptions.assumption: ECDSA with SHA256 and prime256v1 curve remain cryptographically secure
security_assumptions.if_violated: Signature forgery becomes possible, breaking device authentication

security_assumptions.assumption: Secure session (CASE/PASE) provides confidentiality and integrity for attestation exchange
security_assumptions.if_violated: Man-in-the-middle attacks on attestation become possible

security_assumptions.assumption: Certificate Authorities (PAA, PAI) follow proper issuance procedures and protect private keys
security_assumptions.if_violated: Fraudulent certificates could be issued, compromising entire trust model

security_assumptions.assumption: Basic Information cluster accurately reports device VendorID, ProductID, and SoftwareVersion
security_assumptions.if_violated: Certification Declaration matching fails, device could claim false certification

security_assumptions.assumption: System time is accurate enough for certificate validity period checks
security_assumptions.if_violated: Expired or not-yet-valid certificates could be accepted

security_assumptions.assumption: Revocation information in DCL is updated in timely manner when certificates compromised
security_assumptions.if_violated: Compromised certificates continue to be accepted until revocation data updated

security_assumptions.assumption: Commissioner correctly implements all validation steps without implementation bugs
security_assumptions.if_violated: Validation bypasses possible through buggy implementations

security_assumptions.assumption: VendorID/ProductID encoding is not ambiguous and consistently parsed
security_assumptions.if_violated: Encoding confusion attacks enable cross-vendor or cross-product attacks

security_assumptions.assumption: Certification Declarations are issued only for genuinely certified products
security_assumptions.if_violated: False certification claims possible through fraudulent CDs

security_assumptions.assumption: Device attestation occurs before any security-sensitive operations during commissioning
security_assumptions.if_violated: Unauthenticated devices could perform privileged operations

security_assumptions.assumption: CAs publish CRL updates before nextUpdate timestamp expires or Commissioner understands stale CRL risks
security_assumptions.if_violated: Recent revocations may be missed if CRL stale and Commissioner proceeds with warning

security_assumptions.assumption: Users understand security implications when notified about revocation check bypass or CRL staleness
security_assumptions.if_violated: Users may proceed with commissioning compromised devices without understanding risks

security_assumptions.assumption: Firmware updates are properly certified and reflected in DCL DeviceSoftwareCompliance entries
security_assumptions.if_violated: certificate_id mismatch validation may incorrectly reject legitimate firmware updates or accept uncertified firmware

security_assumptions.assumption: CRLDistributionPoints extension in certificates, if present, is not relied upon by Commissioner
security_assumptions.if_violated: Inconsistent revocation checking if Commissioner uses cert extensions instead of DCL as single source

security_assumptions.assumption: Offline commissioning is only used when DCL truly unavailable, not to bypass revocation checks
security_assumptions.if_violated: Attacker could force offline mode to commission revoked devices

validation_checklist.comment: Timer modeling: Timeout is modeled as single transition (AttestationRequested->RequestTimeout) triggered by timeout_expired event. No complex timer state machine needed for this procedure.
